title: ranunculus borealis subsp. turneri
intro: Ranunculus borealis subsp. turneri, also known as the Arctic buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus borealis plant, native to the Arctic regions of North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus borealis subsp. turneri include its ability to aid in the healing process of wounds, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a salve or tincture, which can be applied topically to affected areas or taken orally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
